posts.text: A new book argues that American society privileges the elderly, blocks the young and “is more set on preservation than on renovation”. When it comes to politics, he has a point https://www.economist.com/culture/2026/06/25/is-america-becoming-a-gerontocracy?taid=6a3feed727dbeb0001f31170&utm_campaign=trueanthem&utm_medium=social&utm_source=twitter
posts.author: TheEconomist

posts.text: Retail investors appear to be rotating out of gold and Bitcoin into semiconductor stocks: Since April, US gold and Bitcoin ETFs have posted -$12 billion in cumulative outflows. Over the same period, US semiconductor ETFs have attracted +$20 billion in cumulative inflows. This trend accelerated in mid-May, with outflows from gold and Bitcoin funds more than tripling. At the same time, inflows into semiconductor ETFs have doubled. Meanwhile, the largest US gold-backed ETF, $GLD, is down -13% since the start of April, while the largest Bitcoin ETF, $IBIT, is down -12%. Over the same period, the semiconductor ETFs, $SOXX and $SMH, are up +81% and +60%, respectively. Retail is driving markets like never before.
posts.author: KobeissiLetter
